title: Hyparrhenia jaegeriana

Further classification places it within the subfamily Panicoideae and the tribe Paniceae. At the genus level, Hyparrhenia jaegeriana is a species within the genus Hyparrhenia. intro: Hyparrhenia jaegeriana, commonly known as African feather grass or jaeger's grass, is a perennial grass species native to tropical Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hyparrhenia jaegeriana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and ointments, with the most common herbal preparations including teas and topical creams for wound healing and skin infections.
